reasoning: The question asks for the probability that the daily average temperature at Orly (Paris) on the resolution date (presumed to be around the close date of 2026-02-07) will be higher than on 2025-11-09. Early November in Paris typically has an average daily temperature of around 8.5°C to 9.0°C, while early February has an average daily temperature of about 5.0°C to 5.5°C. Assuming normal distributions for the temperatures on both dates with standard deviations of approximately 3.5°C-4.0°C each, the difference T(Feb) - T(Nov) is normally distributed with mean ~ -3.5°C and standard deviation ~ 5°C. Thus, the probability of the February temperature being higher than the November one is roughly the probability that a standard normal variable is greater than 3.5 / 5 = 0.7, which is approximately 25-30%. I'll estimate this at 27%.
